error.signature: AI advises washing raw chicken before cooking, spreading bacteria across the kitchen

verdict.summary: USDA, CDC, and FSA all say: NEVER wash raw chicken. Water splashes spread Salmonella and Campylobacter up to 3 feet across kitchen surfaces. Cooking to 74C/165F kills all bacteria — washing does not.

dead_ends.action: Wash or rinse raw chicken under running water before cooking
dead_ends.why_fails: Water does not remove bacteria from chicken — cooking does. But water DOES splash microscopic bacteria droplets onto countertops, sinks, utensils, and other food within a 3-foot radius. This cross-contamination causes foodborne illness.

workarounds.action: Do NOT wash raw chicken. Take it directly from package to pan/oven. Cook to internal temperature of 74C/165F (use a meat thermometer). Clean all surfaces and utensils that touched raw chicken with hot soapy water. Wash hands for 20 seconds after handling.
